SHERIFF M CHAEL HENNESSEY
554-7225
City and County of San Francisco
333 City Hall .': 
Sgn Francisco 94102
April 9, 1990
Ar: /ul
b 1; ",r 
'go
Mr. John wallace
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 
95804
Dear John;
Thank you for taking time to talk with me last week about
vrhether or not an elected official 
may use his campaign funds to
sponsor fundraising events and pay dues to political clubs and
community organi zations .
Sheriff Hennessey has recently been asked by the Affordable
Housing Alliance and the San Francisco County Democratic Central
comnittee to sponsor their 
annual fundraising dinners. Elected
officials receive dozens of these requests throughout the year.
Typically, a sponsorship costs more than the regular ticket
price and entitles 
the sponsor to being listed on the event
program. 
we were concerned that 
because many of the
organizations making such requests use the money raised to
support candidates for public office, a sponsorship paid for with
campaign funds might violate the Political 
Reform Actrs
prohibition against transferring 
campaign funds from one
candidate to another.
As I 
understand our conversation, Sheriff Hennessey may use
canpaign funds to pay for sponsorships so long as we indicate
thai ttre contribution must be earmarked for non-contribution
purposes onIy. That is, the organizatj,on may use the Sheriff's
conlribution to pay general operating costs, but may not use it
to support or oppose a candidate.
The same rule would apply to annual dues
democratic clubs and community organizations.
As we discussed, we will compose a short notice
with each sponsorship or dues payment explaining that
received from us must be earmarked.
Thanks for your helP.
Sincerely,
. 
_ll

sections.question_synthetic: May an elected official use campaign funds to sponsor fundraising events and pay dues to political clubs and community organizations, particularly when those organizations use funds to support candidates?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming correspondence/follow-up letter from a requester, not an FPPC advice letter response. The document summarizes a prior verbal conversation with FPPC staff about campaign fund usage. No formal CONCLUSION or QUESTION sections present; structured as informal business letter. The actual FPPC response/advice was given verbally and is only referenced here.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: May an elected official use campaign funds to sponsor fundraising events and pay dues to political clubs and community organizations, particularly when those organizations use funds to support candidates?

O. Box 807 Sacramento, California 95804 Dear John; Thank you for taking time to talk with me last week about vrhether or not an elected official may use his campaign funds to sponsor fundraising events and pay dues to political clubs and community organi zations . Sheriff Hennessey has recently been asked by the Affordable Housing Alliance and the San Francisco County Democratic Central comnittee to sponsor their annual fundraising dinners. Elected officials receive dozens of these requests throughout the year. Typically, a sponsorship costs more than the regular ticket price and entitles the sponsor to being listed on the event program. we were concerned that because many of the organizations making such requests use the money raised to support candidates for public office, a sponsorship paid for with campaign funds might violate the Political Reform Actrs prohibition against transferring campaign funds from one candidate to another. As I understand our conversation, Sheriff Hennessey may use canpaign funds to pay for sponsorships so long as we indicate thai ttre contribution must be earmarked for non-contribution purposes onIy. That is, the organizatj,on may use the Sheriff's conlribution to pay general operating costs, but may not use it to support or oppose a candidate. The same rule would apply to annual dues democratic clubs and community organizations. As we discussed, we will compose a short notice with each sponsorship or dues payment explaining that received from us must be earmarked. Thanks for your helP. Sincerely, . _ll t ( ttt r -:[fc F<\ Eileen Hirst Trea surer Committee to Re-EIect Michael Hennessey Friends of Michael Hennessey (NOT PRINTED AT PUBLIC EXPENSE) e to various to be sent the money
embedding.summary: This is a follow-up letter from Sheriff Hennessey's treasurer to FPPC staff confirming the informal advice received regarding permissible uses of campaign funds for sponsorships and dues payments, with the requirement that contributions be earmarked for non-candidate support purposes.
